subject: A quantitative modeling writeup produced by @quant using skills/finance/ (QUANT.md + MODELS.md, plus PREDICTION.md/MICROSTRUCTURE.md when the goal touches a binary contract or execution mechanics) -- a pricing, risk, or portfolio analysis reported to a client or an @advisor dispatch.

dimensions.desc: Names the specific model used (Black-Scholes, Heston, Merton, Vasicek, CAPM, ...) and states why it fits this question over its alternatives -- not a price asserted with no named model behind it.

States the model's assumptions explicitly (constant volatility, frictionless trading, no early exercise, ...) and checks them against the actual instrument or situation rather than asserting they apply.
Reports a distribution or uncertainty band around the central estimate (a confidence interval, a Greeks-propagated range, VaR/Expected Shortfall) rather than a single point number with no dispersion.
Every figure traces to a toolkit call (mcp__plugin_myfi_myfi-toolkit__quote, the db_* verbs) or a named, checkable input -- no unsourced or invented number.
States what the model does not capture and where it could be wrong (a tail risk the model misses, an assumption under strain, an upcoming event) -- not an upside-only pitch.

guidance: Reward a writeup that names and defends its model choice, states and checks its assumptions against the real instrument, reports a range or distribution instead of a bare point estimate, sources every number to real toolkit data, and states plainly what the model does not capture. Penalize a writeup that asserts a price with no named model, skips or asserts unchecked assumptions, gives only a point estimate with no uncertainty band, contains an unsourced or invented figure, or reads as a one-sided pitch with no stated caveat.
